content:
Key Findings

  • Wyre Council paid £326.5M to LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL between 2017/18 and 2025/26
  • This single supplier received 47.8% of Wyre's total £683.0M spend
  • The top 5 suppliers accounted for £583.3M or 85.4% of total spend
  • BALFOUR BEATTY CIVIL ENGINEERING LTD was the highest private sector supplier at £39.2M
  • £81.6M (11.9%) of spending was recorded as UNKNOWN

The Dominance of Lancashire County Council

Wyre Council's spending data reveals a striking concentration of payments to LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL, which received £326.5M over eight financial years. This represents nearly half (47.8%) of Wyre's total expenditure of £683.0M across 51,092 transactions. The scale of this relationship suggests significant inter-council collaboration or service provision.

The next largest recipient was the DEPARTMENT FOR COMMUNITIES AND LOCAL GOVERNMENT with £116.3M (17.0%). This pattern of large payments to central government departments is typical for local authorities, reflecting funding flows and centralised services. However, the dominance of Lancashire County Council is unusual in its scale.

Private Sector and Public Sector Suppliers

The data shows a mix of public and private sector suppliers. The highest private sector recipient was BALFOUR BEATTY CIVIL ENGINEERING LTD with £39.2M (5.7%). This suggests significant investment in civil engineering projects, potentially for infrastructure or maintenance work. Other notable private sector suppliers include VEOLIA ES (UK) LTD with £16.7M (2.4%) and F PARKINSON LTD with £5.7M (0.8%).

Public sector organisations also feature prominently. The LANCASHIRE FIRE AUTHORITY received £19.9M (2.9%), while the POLICE AND CRIME COMMISSIONER FOR LANCASHIRE received £4.3M (0.6%). These payments likely reflect shared service agreements or funding contributions to regional public safety services.

Unknown Spending and Transparency

A notable portion of Wyre's spending, £81.6M (11.9%), was recorded as UNKNOWN. This category represents a significant share of total expenditure and raises questions about transparency. While some unknown entries may be due to data entry errors or reclassification, such a large sum warrants further scrutiny to ensure accountability.

The top 10 suppliers combined received £616.3M (90.2%) of Wyre's total spend, leaving just £66.7M (9.8%) for all other suppliers. This concentration highlights the importance of monitoring major contracts and ensuring value for money in public spending.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Wyre Council about the nature of payments to LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about major contracts and spending decisions
  • Check the council's published contracts register for details on agreements with suppliers like BALFOUR BEATTY CIVIL ENGINEERING LTD
  • Write to your local councillor about the £81.6M in unknown spending and request clarification
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse trends in your local area
This article is based on Wyre Council's published spending data covering 2017/18 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
